title: vangueriella letestui
intro: Vangueriella letestui, also known as Okoume, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and stems, which are rich in alkaloids and flavonoids. These compounds have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating malaria, and soothing gastrointestinal issues. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Vangueriella letestui are prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
